plant_audit.P2_Mexican_Mint: No visible foliage. Pot contains only substrate.
plant_audit.P3_Pothos: The white rabbit anchor is visible, but the Pothos specimen is missing or entirely desiccated.
plant_audit.P4_Silver_Guest: No visible foliage. Pot appears empty.

biome_observations.debris: White ceramic/plastic fragments (rabbit anchor) are the only non-soil elements.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. There is no evidence of photosynthesis or plant life. The environment is effectively a collection of empty pots.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the complete absence of the registered botanical specimens across all pots.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a chronological audit of the provided image stack. My first step was to establish the baseline registry against the visual data. Upon validation, it is clear that the botanical specimens are missing. The 'White Rabbit' anchor is the only consistent feature. The environment shows no signs of active plant life, suggesting a total systemic loss of the intended biome.
